summary: FrequencyVerse is a client-first, React + TypeScript meditation/relaxation web app centered on playing healing tone frequencies and curated sound sets (kids sleep sounds and peaceful ambient sounds). It provides a unified playback state (single item, session, or playlist), a mobile-optimized main player UI, mood-based recommendations, themeable animated backgrounds, and a full-screen canvas visualization driven by audio intensity. The app supports 11 languages with extensive UI localization and persists user preferences (language, theme, fullscreen preference, background ambiance selection, and audio activation) in localStorage. Project direction update: the experience must be fully anonymous with no login/authentication requirement (remove/disable Internet Identity usage if present).

existing_features.title: Unified main playback state (frequency/sound, queue, modes)
existing_features.description: Provides a MainPlayerProvider context that tracks currentFrequency/currentSoundId, playing/paused flags, playMode (single/session/playlist), repeat/shuffle flags, a typed queue, currentIndex, and duration-related fields. Exposes controls for playFrequency, playSound, playQueue, pause/resume/stop, next/previous, shuffle/repeat toggles, and index updates.

existing_features.title: Sleep timer / auto-stop after selected duration
existing_features.description: MainPlayerProvider starts an internal timer when playback begins and fully resets playback state when selectedDuration elapses, stopping all audio state and clearing queue/session info.

existing_features.title: Mobile-optimized main player UI with playback controls
existing_features.description: MainPlayer component renders a fixed bottom player with play/pause, next/previous, repeat, shuffle, duration selector button, playlist button, and fullscreen visualization toggle, with theme-adaptive styling and touch-friendly sizing.

existing_features.title: Duration selector popup (1–180 minutes)
existing_features.description: DurationSelectorPopup provides a slider-based UI to select playback duration in minutes, with click-outside and ESC-to-close behavior, and theme-adaptive styling.

existing_features.title: Playlist management (add/remove, reorder, shuffle, clear) and playlist popup UI
existing_features.description: useUnifiedPlaylistManager enables building a mixed queue of frequency and sound items, prevents duplicates, supports start/stop/pause/resume timing logic for playlist playback, and integrates with MainPlayer queue. PlaylistPopup displays the queue, allows play item, remove, move up/down, shuffle, start, and clear actions.

existing_features.title: Guided meditation sessions with predefined sequences
existing_features.description: useUnifiedSessionManager defines predefined sessions (healing, chakra, deep meditation, quick relax) and manages session lifecycle (start/pause/resume/stop) by driving the MainPlayer queue/index progression over the selected duration. UnifiedSessionPanel displays available sessions and active session progress with controls.

existing_features.title: Mood Space with mood selection and personalized recommendations
existing_features.description: MoodSpace lets users select from seven moods and shows RecommendedSounds driven by moodRecommendations. Recommendations support play, add-to-playlist (with temporary check feedback), and start-session actions (for frequency items).

existing_features.title: Content catalogs for frequencies, kids sleep sounds, and peaceful sounds
existing_features.description: Defines curated sets: 11 healing frequencies (111–963 Hz) with localized descriptions, kids sleep sounds grouped by category, and peaceful ambient sounds. These are presented in UnifiedFrequencyList using FrequencyCard, KidsSleepSoundCard, and PeacefulSoundCard components, each supporting play/stop and add-to-playlist actions.

existing_features.title: Full-screen mystical sound visualization (canvas)
existing_features.description: SoundWaveVisualization renders a full-screen canvas animation with frequency-specific visual presets and intensity-based pulsing. Supports tap/click to exit and ESC key to close. Visualization fullscreen state is persisted via VisualizationProvider.

existing_features.title: Theme system with animated backgrounds and theme-aware UI styling
existing_features.description: VisualThemeProvider supports five themes (aurora-glow, celestial-calm, sacred-lotus, ethereal-waves, zen-garden) persisted to localStorage. ThemeBackground applies theme-specific background images, animated gradients, and an intensity-driven pulse overlay.

existing_features.title: Language system with 11-language localization and language selector popup
existing_features.description: LanguageProvider manages the active language (tr/en/es/fr/de/it/ru/ar/ja/zh/pt) and exposes a comprehensive translation object. LanguageSelectorPopup provides a theme-styled UI to select language with viewport-aware positioning and localStorage persistence.

existing_features.title: Background ambiance system with automatic fade in/out around main playback
existing_features.description: BackgroundAmbianceProvider manages a selected ambiance type persisted in localStorage and uses HTMLAudioElement + Web Audio gain node to loop and fade ambiance in when the main player is idle and fade it out when main playback starts. BackgroundAmbianceSelectorPopup provides the UI selection modal.

existing_features.title: Audio activation/unlock flow for mobile browsers
existing_features.description: AudioActivationProvider listens for the first touch/click to create/resume an AudioContext and play a near-silent oscillator, enabling subsequent playback; shows a brief ripple indicator at the interaction location and persists activation state in localStorage.

existing_features.title: Unified audio manager with Android native delegation and Web Audio fallback
existing_features.description: useUnifiedAudioManager provides play/stop/volume/intensity tracking. It attempts to use window.AndroidAudio.playSound for native Android playback, and falls back to Web Audio oscillator-based playback while also computing an analyser-driven intensity value for visuals.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication context and backend actor creation
existing_features.description: InternetIdentityProvider wraps AuthClient login/logout and persists/loads identities. useActor creates an anonymous or authenticated canister actor via createActorWithConfig and invalidates/refetches dependent queries when identity changes.

existing_features.title: Motoko blob-storage authorization and cycles refill utilities
existing_features.description: backend/blob-storage includes Storage.mo and Mixin.mo implementing authorized gateway principal updates, dead blob inspection/pruning, and cashier refill logic using cycles. backend/main.mo is intentionally empty for this app.

existing_features.title: Startup diagnostics + app-level error boundary to avoid blank-screen failures
existing_features.description: Adds startupDiagnostics instrumentation and an AppErrorBoundary wrapper to surface runtime initialization/render errors (instead of a white/blank screen) and provide basic diagnostic logging during app startup.

existing_features.title: Centralized AndroidAudio bridge wrapper with defensive native-vs-web routing
existing_features.description: Adds a dedicated TypeScript bridge module (e.g., src/lib/androidAudioBridge.ts) to centralize calls to window.AndroidAudio, defensively checking method existence (playSound/stopSound) and catching invocation errors. On any missing-method or invocation failure, it falls back to the existing web audio path to prevent runtime crashes and preserve playback.

existing_features.title: Android native soundId normalization/mapping for res/raw playback
existing_features.description: Introduces a dedicated soundId normalization layer for Android native playback (e.g., src/lib/androidNativeSoundId.ts) to map/alias web-catalog sound IDs to the exact Android res/raw resource names (no extension). Used by the AndroidAudio bridge/audio manager to reduce native playback failures caused by naming mismatches (e.g., ninni_kalimba vs kalimba_ninni).

existing_features.title: Fix the Player’s non-working “Animation” section/tab so it reliably renders while audio is playing, without causing runtime errors or blank screens.

Implement a meditation-style animation system that selects an appropriate animation based on the currently playing item (frequency or soundId). Use a centralized mapping (e.g., soundId → animationType; frequency → animationType) so every supported sound can have a consistent animation style.

Update the fullscreen visualization experience so the meditation animations are truly fullscreen (cover the entire viewport), resize correctly on orientation changes, and remain visually comfortable (slow movement, low flicker, low contrast strain).
Ensure all user-facing text related to the visualization/animation UI is in English and is routed through the existing localization system (no hardcoded Turkish strings in components).

new_feature_requests.summary: Provide an Android WebView APK wrapper for FrequencyVerse with offline caching (LOAD_CACHE_ELSE_NETWORK) and a JavaScriptInterface bridge named AndroidAudio for playing res/raw sounds
Implement dual-audio routing: if window.AndroidAudio exists use native res/raw playback; otherwise keep and use the existing Web Audio/CDN fallback without breaking frequency playback

Provide a complete Android Studio WebView wrapper project setup (MainActivity, AndroidManifest, Gradle, layout) with offline caching (LOAD_CACHE_ELSE_NETWORK) and a JavaScriptInterface bridge (AndroidAudio) that plays res/raw sounds by soundId (no file extension), while preserving web-audio fallback when AndroidAudio is absent
Kısa süreli ses dosyalarını (res/raw veya web audio) seçilen çalma süresi boyunca otomatik döngüye alarak (loop) tekrar tekrar çalabilme
Add 'Hoparlör Soft Temizlik' and 'Hoparlör Hard Temizlik' items at the top of the Frequencies section (speaker cleaning mode) with full localization support
Add a new 'Sosyal Hayat Frekansları' tab/section under Frequencies with the provided frequency-range items and full localization support
Remove/disable authentication (Internet Identity) and any login-related UI/flows so the app is fully anonymous
Remove specific sound items from catalogs/UI: 'hafif_fisilti', 'piyano_ninni', and 'masal_ambiyansi'
Fix mobile layout overlap with Android status bar and navigation bar (safe-area/window insets) in the WebView APK + web UI so top/bottom UI is not hidden under system bars

Implement gapless looping (or mitigation like crossfade/pre-scheduling) when repeating short audio files to fill long selected durations, for both Android native (res/raw/MediaPlayer) and web-audio fallback
Fix the player 'animation' tab/section not working and implement per-sound, meditation-style animations that match each sound name, including proper fullscreen behavior

architectural_notes: Frontend uses React Context Providers for cross-app state: LanguageProvider, VisualThemeProvider, VisualizationProvider (fullscreen state), MainPlayerProvider (unified playback state + sleep timer), BackgroundAmbianceProvider, AudioActivationProvider, and InternetIdentityProvider.
Playback is modeled as a queue of typed items: {type:'frequency', id:number} and {type:'sound', id:string}, allowing sessions and playlists to share the same player state shape.
UI is componentized with TailwindCSS (including a custom xs breakpoint) and shared UI primitives (Button, Card, Slider).
Full-screen visualization is implemented via an imperative HTML canvas animation loop (requestAnimationFrame) with frequency-specific visual configuration.
User preferences and feature state are persisted using localStorage keys (language, theme, fullscreen mode, ambiance selection, audio activation).
Audio unlocking for mobile browsers is handled by creating/resuming an AudioContext and playing a near-silent oscillator on first user interaction.
React Query is used for actor creation and query invalidation when identity changes; overall app functionality is largely client-side without backend queries.
Backend includes a Motoko blob-storage module/mixin for authorization and cycles refill flow via a 'cashier' principal; backend main actor is intentionally empty.

Mobile (Android WebView APK) plan: support dual audio backends—(1) native Android `res/raw` playback via a JavaScriptInterface named `AndroidAudio` (MediaPlayer) and (2) existing web audio/CDN playback as fallback. Rule: if `window.AndroidAudio` exists use native; otherwise use web fallback. `addJavascriptInterface` must be called before `loadUrl`, and JS calls `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` without file extension.

Project now includes a reusable build-template scaffold (Dockerfile, deploy scripts, canister.yaml, workspace/package configs) intended to standardize builds/deploys for the React frontend + ICP backend.
Shadcn/ui component library has been introduced/expanded in the build-template (large set of prebuilt UI primitives under src/components/ui) for consistent UI construction.
Custom ESLint rules have been added in the build-template to enforce project-specific constraints (e.g., avoiding BigInt in React Query keys; requiring InternetIdentityProvider usage patterns).
Android WebView APK wrapper should support offline caching via webSettings.cacheMode = WebSettings.LOAD_CACHE_ELSE_NETWORK (avoid blank screen when offline).

Native audio bridge requirements reaffirmed: JavaScriptInterface name must be AndroidAudio; @JavascriptInterface required; addJavascriptInterface must be called before loadUrl; JS should call window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId) with no file extension.

Dual audio rule: native and web audio backends must coexist—native may replace web playback when available, but web fallback must remain functional and should be used when native cannot play the requested soundId (e.g., frequency oscillator tones).

Android WebView wrapper implementation direction: WebView loads the hosted FrequencyVerse URL and registers a JavaScriptInterface named 'AndroidAudio' (added before loadUrl) exposing playSound(soundId) / stopSound(soundId) that maps soundId -> res/raw resource via getIdentifier; soundId must be passed without file extension.

Dual-audio rule reaffirmed with explicit pseudocode requirement on web side: if (window.AndroidAudio) window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId) else playWebAudio(soundId); web fallback must remain enabled for frequencies that are not backed by res/raw assets.
Offline requirement for the WebView wrapper: webSettings.cacheMode must be WebSettings.LOAD_CACHE_ELSE_NETWORK to avoid blank screen when offline.

Added startup diagnostics (startupDiagnostics) and an app-level React error boundary (AppErrorBoundary) to catch/render runtime errors and improve visibility into startup crashes that previously resulted in a blank/white screen.

Android WebView APK: WebView must register JS bridge `AndroidAudio` via `addJavascriptInterface(..., "AndroidAudio")` before `loadUrl()`. Web-side audio routing must follow: if (window.AndroidAudio) window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId) else playWebAudio(soundId); web fallback must remain enabled (especially for oscillator-based frequencies).

Android JS bridge must expose methods that exactly match web calls; current web code invokes AndroidAudio.stopSound(...) and will throw 'Method not found' if the JavaScriptInterface is registered under a different name or does not define @JavascriptInterface fun stopSound(name:String).

Android WebView bridge contract must match exactly: web calls window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId) and window.AndroidAudio.stopSound(soundId) (no file extension); Android must expose @JavascriptInterface methods with those exact names/signatures or web must guard calls.

Android WebView native-audio bridge must expose both @JavascriptInterface playSound(name:String) and stopSound(name:String) (or defensively guard stopSound on web) to avoid 'Method not found' errors that can trigger React ErrorBoundary and break playback.

Add a web-side TypeScript 'AndroidAudio' bridge/wrapper module to centralize native vs web-audio routing with defensive guards: if (window.AndroidAudio?.playSound) try native play/stop; on missing methods/errors fall back to existing Web Audio/CDN path (do not disable fallback).

Add an Android native soundId normalization/mapping module (e.g., src/lib/androidNativeSoundId.ts) that converts/aliases catalog sound IDs to exact Android res/raw resource names (no extension) before calling window.AndroidAudio.playSound/stopSound, preventing failures due to naming mismatches between web catalogs and Android resources.

Web audio routing now includes a native soundId mapping/alias step (e.g., webId "white-noise" -> nativeId "white_noise") and emits debug logs like: "[AudioManager] Native routing: webId=..., nativeId=..., explicitMapping=..., bridgeSuccess=..." to confirm mapping/bridge invocation.

DevTools console scope note: `soundId` and `audioContext` are not globals; testing must call `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('id')` with a literal string, and AudioContext state cannot be inspected unless explicitly exposed on `window`.

Android res/raw resource naming constraint: filenames (resource names) must be lowercase a-z, 0-9, or underscore only; hyphens '-' are invalid and will fail resource merge/build. Web soundId normalization/mapping should account for this (e.g., music-box -> muzik_kutusu).
Product requirement: app must be fully anonymous; remove/disable Internet Identity authentication flows/providers if they exist in the build.

Debug/diagnostics finding: white-screen crash was traced to provider tree mismatch error `useUnifiedAudioManagerContext must be used within UnifiedAudioManagerProvider` (e.g., ThemeBackground calling the hook outside the provider). Fix requires ensuring UnifiedAudioManagerProvider wraps any component using the hook.

Android build constraint confirmed in practice: res/raw filenames cannot contain '-' (hyphens). Example failure: `res/raw/music-box.mp3` breaks mergeDebugResources; must rename to underscore form (e.g., `music_box.mp3`) and align soundId normalization/mapping accordingly.

Android resource-name constraint also applies to non-ASCII characters (e.g., Turkish 'ı', 'ş', 'ğ'); raw resource names must be lowercase a-z, 0-9, underscore only. Web soundIds like `anne_karnı_sesi` must be normalized to `anne_karni_sesi` (ASCII) for native playback.

Looping requirement clarified: when a short sound (native res/raw or web audio) is played with a long selected duration (e.g., 180 minutes), playback must automatically loop/restart seamlessly until the timer ends; implement consistently across Android MediaPlayer/native and web fallback.

For long selected durations with short native res/raw sounds, implement looping on Android by setting MediaPlayer.isLooping = true (and removing redundant OnCompletionListener seekTo(0)); duration stopping should be handled by the app timer calling stopSound at the end.

Mobile WebView UX requirement: handle Android status/navigation bar insets (edge-to-edge) so app UI respects safe areas (WindowInsets on native side and/or CSS env(safe-area-inset-*) padding on web side) to avoid content hidden behind system bars.

Refined looping requirement: when short audio files are looped to fill long durations, looping should be gapless (no audible silence/cut between iterations). Implement gapless looping behavior as consistently as possible across Android native (MediaPlayer) and web fallback; if true gapless is not feasible for a backend, mitigate via crossfade or prebuffered/re-scheduled playback.

Confirmed runtime audio path: sounds are played natively on Android (res/raw via MediaPlayer through the AndroidAudio JavaScriptInterface), while playback is triggered from the web (React) side; audio works in the mobile WebView APK when called correctly.

Gapless looping requirement reiterated: when extending short native res/raw sounds to long durations by repeating segments, playback should continue with no audible gap. MediaPlayer restart/onCompletion re-play can introduce unavoidable gaps; consider using a true looping strategy (MediaPlayer.isLooping for single-file loops) or an alternative approach such as dual-player preloading/scheduled handoff, ExoPlayer with seamless looping, or crossfade mitigation for near-gapless transitions.

Android native gapless looping note: MediaPlayer.isLooping=true is gapless for a single file loop; if true gapless is needed while repeating segments or swapping sources, use a more advanced strategy (e.g., dual-player preloading/handoff, ExoPlayer seamless looping, or crossfade mitigation).

Gapless looping decision confirmed: for short Android res/raw sounds that must play for long durations, each sound is a single file and should loop seamlessly using MediaPlayer.isLooping=true (do not use onCompletion restart). Duration stopping remains controlled by the app timer calling stopSound at the end.

Animation/player requirement refinement: fix the non-working Player 'Animation' tab and implement per-sound, meditation-style animations mapped from soundId -> animationType (central mapping). Fullscreen behavior: animation must cover the entire screen, handle resize/safe-area correctly, keep playback running, and use slow/soothing (non-eye-straining) visuals; normal mode can show appropriate animation for the currently playing sound.
known_issues: Time unit inconsistency: MainPlayer.formatTime() expects milliseconds, but UnifiedSessionManager sets totalDuration/timeRemaining in seconds, while UnifiedPlaylistManager uses milliseconds. This can break countdown display and progress calculations.

MainPlayerProvider implements a sleep timer that stops playback after selectedDuration, but it does not decrement timeRemaining; session/playlist managers maintain their own timers, which can conflict with the sleep timer (e.g., session interval may keep running after the sleep timer resets player state).

useUnifiedAudioManager Web Audio fallback only supports parsing frequency from a 'freq-###' soundId; non-frequency sound IDs (e.g., 'white-noise', 'peaceful-light-rain') will not play in web fallback mode because parseInt() fails (and no sample playback is implemented in the shown code).
BackgroundAmbianceProvider has audio.src assignment commented out; without real audio files wired in, ambiance playback will be silent/non-functional.

LanguageSelectorPopup/ThemeSelectorPopup/BackgroundAmbianceSelectorPopup derive the current language by heuristics on t.subtitle text (string contains checks) instead of using the actual language code from useLanguage(), which is fragile and error-prone.
Nested QueryClientProviders exist (in main.tsx and App.tsx) with different QueryClient instances, which is redundant and can cause confusing cache/query behavior.

Some component files are present but empty (e.g., ThemeSelector.tsx, PlaylistPanel.tsx, FrequencyList.tsx, SessionPanel.tsx, EmbeddedVisualization.tsx, UnifiedPlaylistPanel.tsx, BackgroundAmbianceSelector.tsx, LanguageSelector.tsx), suggesting unfinished/legacy code or build-time dead code.
Duplicate CSS entry points exist (frontend/index.css and frontend/src/index.css) with different design tokens; this can lead to styling inconsistencies depending on which file is actually imported.

Audio regression: after introducing dual audio routing (native AndroidAudio vs web fallback), some/ all built-in frequency sounds no longer play. Likely causes: web-side `useUnifiedAudioManager` not calling `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` on user play actions, incorrect branching causing neither backend to trigger, or mismatched sound IDs/filenames (should be ID without .mp3).

Web Audio error observed: `InvalidStateError: createMediaElementSource` called multiple times for the same HTMLMediaElement, causing playback failures; requires ensuring only one MediaElementSourceNode per element or recreating Audio elements / disconnecting nodes.

Mobile/WebView playback issue: manual `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('deniz_dalgasi')` works but UI play button does not, indicating event handler/bridge integration problem (play button path still using WebAudio or wrong soundId).
Severe regression: app shows a blank/white screen and fails to render (reported after recent audio-path separation changes around versions 66–67).
Severe regression persists: app renders a blank/white screen (runtime crash) in recent builds even after attempted rollbacks; needs root-cause isolation (startup crash) and visible diagnostics.

Audio routing regression expanded: after adding AndroidAudio/native routing, legacy frequency playback can stop entirely if fallback gating is wrong (AndroidAudio detected but soundId not playable, and web fallback not executed).

Android native-audio integration gap: manual DevTools call window.AndroidAudio.playSound('deniz_dalgasi') works, but UI play button path may not call AndroidAudio (soundId not routed/triggered from click handlers).

Regression/bug: Only normal (web/oscillator) frequencies play; native `res/raw` sounds do not play. Likely causes: web-side play handlers still call WebAudio path and never invoke `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` for non-frequency sound IDs, or soundId mismatch vs `res/raw` names, or Android JS bridge not available/registered in the running WebView context.

Runtime crash: `Error invoking stopSound: Method not found` occurs when web code calls AndroidAudio.stopSound but the Android JavaScriptInterface lacks a matching @JavascriptInterface stopSound method/signature; triggers ErrorBoundary and can break audio.

Web-side crash/blocked playback risk: When Android JS bridge is present but missing stopSound (or method signature mismatch), web code calling window.AndroidAudio.stopSound(...) throws 'Method not found' and can trigger ErrorBoundary; web should defensively check method existence (and/or Android must implement stopSound) to avoid crashes and allow proper fallback.

Current regression status: app now generally renders/works, but Android native `res/raw` sounds still do not play from the UI (only web/oscillator frequencies work). Likely causes: web-side play handlers not routing to `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` for non-frequency items, bridge method mismatch/availability timing, or soundId mismatch vs `res/raw` resource names.

Android `res/raw` sesleri (native) manuel `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` ile çalabiliyor, ancak UI üzerinden çalmıyor; kök neden büyük olasılıkla web-side play handler'ın `window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId)` çağrısını tetiklememesi (hala WebAudio yoluna gitmesi) ve/veya `stopSound` çağrısının bridge tarafında 'Method not found' hatası vererek ErrorBoundary ile akışı bozması (native çalma hemen kesiliyor veya hiç başlatılamıyor).

User reports res/raw filenames/IDs are correct on Android side, but suspects the web UI is emitting different/incorrect soundId values when pressing Play; needs definitive extraction/verification of actual soundId strings used in web-side play/stop calls (vs manual window.AndroidAudio.playSound(...) working).

Potential soundId mismatch: web UI may be calling English/alternate sound IDs that do not exactly match Android res/raw resource names (expected IDs like 'deniz_dalgasi' without extension), causing native raw sounds not to play even though manual window.AndroidAudio.playSound('...') works.

Regression observed during iterations: app can white-screen/crash due to provider tree mismatch (e.g., 'useUnifiedAudioManagerContext must be used within UnifiedAudioManagerProvider'), requiring strict provider ordering and guarding hook usage.

Android/WebView bridge mismatch can surface as runtime crash: web calls AndroidAudio.stopSound(soundId) but native bridge lacks matching method/signature/@JavascriptInterface, triggering ErrorBoundary and breaking audio/UI.

Potential soundId mismatch discovered: web UI appears to call different IDs than expected for some Kids Sleep sounds (e.g., uses `ninni_kalimba` / `ninni_harp` instead of `kalimba_ninni` / `harp_ninni`, and includes `beyaz_gurultu_hafif` within Kids list). If Android res/raw assets and/or catalogs use the other naming, native playback via AndroidAudio will fail even though manual `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('...')` works for correctly named IDs.

Evidence from web UI soundId extraction: Kids Sleep sounds include `ninni_kalimba` and `ninni_harp` (and also `beyaz_gurultu_hafif`), which may not match Android `res/raw` naming expectations (e.g., `kalimba_ninni` / `harp_ninni`), leading to native raw sounds not playing despite IDs seeming correct.

Raw/res/raw sesleri çalmıyor (manual `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('...')` çalışıyor) ama UI Play’den çalışmıyor; konsolda `Error invoking stopSound: Method not found` görülüyor. Bu, web tarafının `AndroidAudio.stopSound(soundId)` çağırmasına rağmen Android JavaScriptInterface tarafında `stopSound` metodunun bulunmaması/yanlış isim/eksik `@JavascriptInterface` nedeniyle ErrorBoundary tetiklenip native ses akışının kırılmasına yol açabilir.

Web UI soundId listesi çıkarıldı: Kids Sleep’te `ninni_kalimba` ve `ninni_harp` kullanılıyor; Android `res/raw` tarafında dosyalar `kalimba_ninni` / `harp_ninni` gibi farklı adlandırılmışsa native çalma sessizce başarısız olur (getIdentifier 0 döner).

Android/WebView integration: raw/native sounds may not play from UI even though manual DevTools call works, if web play handlers do not route to window.AndroidAudio.playSound(soundId) for Kids Sleep / Peaceful sounds (routing mismatch).

Android/WebView bridge runtime error: 'Error invoking stopSound: Method not found' indicates AndroidAudio JavaScriptInterface is missing stopSound or @JavascriptInterface annotation/signature mismatch; can trigger ErrorBoundary and interrupt audio/UI.

WebView native res/raw sounds may not play from UI even though manual `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('id')` works; root cause likely missing/incorrect web-side TS bridge routing/guards (native vs web fallback) and/or unguarded stopSound calls triggering ErrorBoundary instead of falling back.

Debugging note: In DevTools console, `audioContext` and `soundId` are not defined globals; attempting `audioContext?.state`, `audioContext.resume()`, or `console.log('PLAY', soundId, ...)` directly will throw ReferenceError unless those variables are exposed on `window` by the app. This can confuse diagnosis of Web Audio state and which soundId is being played.

Native/raw sounds may fail even when the bridge works (manual `window.AndroidAudio.playSound('...')` succeeds) if the web UI emits English or hyphenated sound IDs (e.g., `white-noise`) while Android `res/raw` requires underscore IDs (e.g., `white_noise`); inconsistent application of the webId→nativeId normalization/mapping can cause UI play to be silent.

Android build error risk: res/raw filename contains invalid '-' character (e.g., music-box.mp3). Android resource names must use only lowercase letters, digits, and underscores, so such files prevent build (mergeDebugResources FAILED).

Android build error confirmed: `:app:mergeDebugResources FAILED` due to invalid '-' character in res/raw filename (example: `music-box.mp3`). Must rename to underscore-only resource name and ensure webId→nativeId mapping matches.

Native playback naming risk confirmed: some web soundIds may contain non-ASCII Turkish characters (e.g., `anne_karnı_sesi`) which are invalid Android resource names; must normalize to ASCII/underscore (e.g., `anne_karni_sesi`) or native lookup via getIdentifier will fail.

Short-duration native res/raw sounds do not currently loop to fill the selected playback duration (e.g., 180 minutes), causing playback to go silent after the file ends; needs looping/restart behavior tied to the sleep timer/duration selection.
Mobile UI issue: on phones, app content is overlapped/hidden under the Android status bar (top) and navigation bar (bottom); requires proper safe-area / window-insets handling in WebView and/or CSS.

UI bug: Theme and Background Ambiance dropdown/popover panels open upward and overflow off-screen on mobile, making options partially invisible; needs viewport-aware positioning (flip/shift) and/or scrollable max-height.

Looping UX issue: when short audio files are repeated to fill long selected durations, there is an audible brief silence/gap between repeats; needs gapless looping (or mitigation such as crossfade/pre-scheduling).

Android native looping: if implementation relies on MediaPlayer completion/restart or swapping players for long-duration playback, short audible gaps can occur; achieving near-gapless may require ExoPlayer, dual-player scheduled handoff, or crossfade.
Player'daki animasyon sekmesi/alanı çalışmıyor; kullanıcı her ses için ismine uygun meditasyon animasyonları ve tam ekran modda doğru görüntülenme istiyor.
